criteria_results.title: Allegation 1: Cherry-Picking Trade Allocations â€” Identified
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output includes a dedicated section titled 'Cherry-Picking Trade Allocations' that identifies this as a distinct allegation category. It describes the pattern of disproportionately allocating profitable trades to Chao's personal account and Oceanic Fund I LP, mentions Chao's role as Head of Trading and her direct responsibility for allocation decisions, and notes the financial harm to other clients. This satisfies the criterion of identifying cherry-picking/favorable trade allocation as a distinct allegation category with the required details.

criteria_results.title: Cherry-Picking: Correct Statutory Provisions Cited
criteria_results.reasoning: The Cherry-Picking Trade Allocations section does not cite Sections 206(1) or 206(2) of the Investment Advisers Act of 1940. Those provisions are only cited in the Breach of Fiduciary Duty / Directed Brokerage Arrangement section, in connection with Bellini's conduct. The cherry-picking section references the Firm's Brochure misrepresentations and statistical analysis but never identifies the specific statutory provisions (206(1) and/or 206(2)) as the basis for the cherry-picking allegation.

criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output clearly identifies WCA (Whitmore Capital Advisors LLC) as an implicated party in the cherry-picking allegation. The Cherry-Picking Trade Allocations section explicitly references WCA multiple times, including statements about 'The Firm's Brochure falsely represented that allocations were made on a pro rata basis,' references to 'WCA advisory clients,' and mentions of 'the Firm' receiving financial benefit from preferential allocations. The output mentions October 18, 2024 in the Obstruction section, but incorrectly associates it with Whitmore being 'instructed to delete' records rather than the referral to Enforcement. The referral notice date of November 6, 2024 and the service date of November 12, 2024 are not mentioned anywhere in the output. Since fewer than three of the five dates are correctly stated (only two are accurate), the criterion requires a FAIL verdict.

criteria_results.title: Whitmore's 12% LP Interest Value Correctly Stated
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output does not mention Whitmore's 12% limited partnership interest in Oceanic Fund I LP or its valuation of approximately $22.2 million (12% × $185M) anywhere in the document. This specific fact is entirely absent from the allegation summary.

criteria_results.title: Compliance Manual Last Updated November 15, 2022
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output explicitly mentions 'WCA's written policies and procedures, as documented in their Compliance Manual (last updated November 15, 2022)' in the Deficient Written Policies and Procedures section.

criteria_results.title: Lakeshore Trading LLC: Ostrowski's 40% Ownership Stated
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output mentions that Marcus T. Bellini directed client trades to Lakeshore Trading LLC based on his 'undisclosed personal relationship with a 40% owner of that firm,' but it does not identify that person as Kevin Ostrowski. The criterion requires that the output identify Lakeshore Trading LLC as 40% owned by Kevin Ostrowski specifically. Since Ostrowski's identity as the 40% owner is omitted, the criterion is not satisfied.

criteria_results.title: Lakeshore Trading LLC: Ostrowski-Bellini Personal Relationship and Undisclosed Nature
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output mentions that Bellini directed client trades to Lakeshore Trading LLC based on an 'undisclosed personal relationship with a 40% owner of that firm,' but it never identifies that the 40% owner is Ostrowski or that Ostrowski is Bellini's college roommate. The criterion specifically requires identification of Ostrowski as Bellini's college roommate and that this relationship was undisclosed. The output omits the specific nature of the personal relationship (college roommate) and the identity of Ostrowski in connection with this relationship.

criteria_results.title: Email Exhibit Language Quoted or Paraphrased
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output does not reference the Whitmore-to-Delgado email (whitmore-delgado-email.eml) at all, nor does it mention the ambiguous language such as 'clean up the shared drive' or 'clean up of legacy files on the shared drive' that could support an innocent interpretation. The obstruction section discusses Whitmore's instruction to delete records but does not cite or quote the specific email exhibit or note its potentially ambiguous language.
